steps.title: Establish the pay structure
steps.content: For the first 40 hours, the employee earns \$28/hr, yielding 40(\$28) = \$1,120. For each hour beyond 40, the rate is \$42/hr. The goal is at least \$1,510.

steps.title: Backsolve the candidates
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (49): The employee works 49 - 40 = 9 hours of overtime at \$42/hr, yielding \$1,120 + 9(\$42) = \$1,120 + \$378 = \$1,498.

Since \$1,498 < \$1,510, B fails.

Testing choice A (50): The employee works 50 - 40 = 10 hours of overtime at \$42/hr, yielding \$1,120 + 10(\$42) = \$1,120 + \$420 = \$1,540.

Since \$1,540 ≥ \$1,510, the target is met. That confirms A is correct.
